exampleSentences: Mỗi sáng em vào lớp đều chào thầy rất lễ phép.
Khi gặp thầy ở ngoài đường, anh luôn nhớ chào thầy trước.
Chị dặn em phải chào thầy khi đến trường mới.
notes:
“chào thầy” means “hello, teacher” or “greet the (male) teacher.” It’s what students say to a male teacher to show respect.

chào = to greet, thầy = male teacher (also used for male master/mentor in some contexts).  
You use it directly when meeting him, usually with polite tone and body language (slight bow or nod).  

For a female teacher, say chào cô — easy pair to remember.
notes_md:
**“chào thầy”** means “hello, teacher” or “greet the (male) teacher.” It’s what students say to a male teacher to show respect.

**chào** = to greet, **thầy** = male teacher (also used for male master/mentor in some contexts).  
You use it directly when meeting him, usually with polite tone and body language (slight bow or nod).  


For a female teacher, say **chào cô** — easy pair to remember.
exampleSentencesTranslated: Every morning when I enter the class, I always greet teacher politely.
When meeting teacher on the street, I always remember to greet teacher first.
She reminded me to greet teacher when arriving at the new school.
